mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly momentum in PAYEMS signals rising household income and stronger aggregate demand, which lifts expected corporate revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has nonpositive correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year walk-forward window or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in FEDFUNDS marks tighter discount-rate conditions that reduce equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the crossing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward events or mean triggered return is not below unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break events do not produce lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-event months, or if event hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL combined with rising FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure forcing policy tightening, which raises discount rates and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase signal has no lower 20-day return than either variable alone, or if interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with falling UNRATE identifies a broad real-activity expansion that improves operating leverage and earnings expectations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive months and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up and UNRATE-down state is not followed by higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than baseline, or if positive-return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, leading investors to mark down future earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise events are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average event return is not below unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: Positive short-run momentum in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 1-month or 3-month growth fails to produce positive Rank IC versus 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and UNRATE is elevated, policy support dominates discount-rate risk; declining UNRATE in that regime predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and UNRATE is above 6.5% while UNRATE declines over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if declining UNRATE under FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and UNRATE above 6.5% does not outperform unconditional 60-day returns, or if the interaction term is not positive.
